meta.purpose: Real LLM A/B: does reading triage-validation/SKILL.md improve verdict + severity accuracy vs an unaided security-savvy baseline?
meta.ground_truth_source: skills/triage-validation/SKILL.md NEVER SUBMIT list + CONDITIONALLY VALID table + Q7 precedence + CVSS quick reference
meta.scoring: verdict exact match = 1pt; severity exact match = 1pt; per case max 2pt

cases.title: Open redirect standalone
cases.scenario: GET /redirect?url=https://evil.com returns 302 Location: https://evil.com. No OAuth flow, no token in redirect, no sensitive navigation demonstrated. Just an open redirect on a marketing page.
cases.rationale: Open redirect alone is on NEVER SUBMIT; no chain, no concrete next hop demonstrated.

cases.title: Open redirect chained into OAuth code theft (proven end-to-end)
cases.scenario: The /redirect?url= endpoint is reachable as the OAuth redirect_uri. PoC shows the authorization code is delivered to attacker.com and exchanged for a victim access token, yielding full account takeover. Full chain reproduced with raw requests.
cases.rationale: Open redirect + OAuth redirect_uri code theft, chain proven end to end = ATO Critical (REPORT, not CHAIN_REQUIRED).

cases.title: Open redirect, OAuth reachable, chain NOT yet built
cases.scenario: Open redirect confirmed. The same host also runs an OAuth flow and the redirect_uri validation looks loose, but the tester has NOT yet demonstrated code/token theft. There is a concrete next hop to try.
cases.rationale: Chain-eligible, concrete next hop exists but unbuilt = CHAIN_REQUIRED (not DO_NOT_REPORT, not REPORT).

cases.title: Self-XSS only
cases.scenario: User can inject <script> into their own profile bio, which executes only in their own session. No way shown to deliver it to another user.
cases.rationale: Self-XSS alone is on NEVER SUBMIT; no victim-delivery chain.

cases.title: SSRF DNS-only callback
cases.scenario: A URL parameter triggers a DNS lookup to attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator, but no HTTP response body is returned, no internal service reached, no data read back.
cases.rationale: SSRF DNS callback only, no internal access/data = NEVER SUBMIT standalone.

cases.title: SSRF reaching cloud metadata with credential read-back
cases.scenario: The fetch parameter reaches http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ and the response body containing temporary IAM credentials is reflected back to the attacker. Reproduced with raw request/response.
cases.rationale: SSRF to cloud metadata with data returned; the reference vector scores 10.0 Critical.

cases.title: CORS wildcard reflection without credentials
cases.scenario: Server reflects arbitrary Origin into Access-Control-Allow-Origin, but Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is false and the endpoint returns only public, non-sensitive data.
cases.rationale: CORS wildcard without credential exfil PoC = NEVER SUBMIT.

cases.title: CORS misconfig with credentialed PII exfiltration (proven)
cases.scenario: Server reflects attacker Origin AND sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true on /api/me. PoC page hosted on attacker.com reads the victim's full profile (email, address, phone) cross-origin with cookies. Demonstrated end to end.
cases.rationale: CORS + credentialed sensitive response exfil, proven = High.

cases.title: Missing security headers
cases.scenario: Scanner reports the site is missing Content-Security-Policy and HSTS headers. No exploit demonstrated.
cases.rationale: Missing CSP/HSTS on NEVER SUBMIT; not chain-eligible.

cases.title: IDOR reading other users' PII
cases.scenario: GET /api/orders/{id} lets an authenticated free user increment id to read any other user's order details: name, shipping address, items. Two accounts used to prove cross-user read. No write.
cases.rationale: IDOR read PII, auth required, any user; CVSS 6.5 Medium.

cases.title: IDOR allowing write/delete on other users' objects
cases.scenario: DELETE /api/documents/{id} succeeds against documents owned by other users; attacker (free account) can delete any user's documents. Proven with two accounts.
cases.rationale: IDOR write/delete any user; the reference vector scores 8.1 High.

cases.title: Authentication bypass to admin panel
cases.scenario: Setting a cookie role=admin (no server-side verification) grants full access to /admin including user management and config. No login as admin required. Reproduced from a fresh unauthenticated session.
cases.rationale: Auth bypass to admin; CVSS 9.8 Critical.

cases.title: Host header injection + password reset poisoning (proven)
cases.scenario: Changing the Host header on POST /forgot-password causes the reset email link sent to the victim to point at attacker-controlled host, leaking the victim's reset token when they click. Demonstrated with a real reset email capture.
cases.rationale: Host header injection + reset poisoning chain proven = High.

cases.title: GraphQL introspection enabled, nothing else shown
cases.scenario: Introspection query returns the full schema. No field-level auth bypass, no IDOR on node(), no sensitive mutation demonstrated.
cases.rationale: GraphQL introspection alone on NEVER SUBMIT; no demonstrated bypass.
